title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC to support social workers.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 to aid Boston Medical Center's mission of providing care to patients regardless of their ability to pay.
Inspired by her mother and roommate, Beattie emphasizes the critical role of social workers in healthcare at BMC.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave, remaining winless in their NWSL debut season.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized the need for patience and better execution in the final third of the field.
Star forward Amanda Gutierres, despite active participation, failed to score and had low passing accuracy.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr. charged in Malden District Court for embezzling client funds and unauthorized law practice.
Prosecutors allege Rappa misused $660,000 meant for a client trust account, violating court suspensions.
Rappa, 57, ordered to surrender passport, banned from witness contact; bail set at $2,500.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a fiery crash near Bedford Toll Plaza, New Hampshire, suffering serious injuries.
Mirman thanked rescuers, including a state trooper and Governor Kelly Ayotte's security detail, via an Instagram post.
No charges filed; the crash, involving his 2026 Lucid Gravity, is under investigation with police seeking information.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: An unprovoked attack in Dover led to the arrest of 53-year-old Jonathan G. Newcomb for assaulting a NH State Trooper.
The incident occurred as the trooper worked in his cruiser; Newcomb allegedly punched him and tried to take his gun.
Newcomb faces multiple charges, including felony criminal threatening, and was treated for minor injuries before his arraignment.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, runs the Boston Marathon again to honor his late second mother Sharla Sieve.
Sieve passed from metastatic colorectal cancer in December 2022; her fundraiser raised over $123,000 for Mass General.
Mallett, a Boston Bruins videographer, aims to complete all major marathons, with Boston being significant due to its local importance.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to trade for Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1.
The Eagles seek significant draft compensation, including a first-round pick, in a potential A.J. Brown trade.
The Patriots maintain a strong interest in Brown but have not confirmed any under-the-table deal with Philadelphia.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' a musical film streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' new documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' streams on PBS, featuring voices of Jeff Goldblum and Meryl Streep.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeat Padres 5-2 in home opener, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin breaks out of an 0-for-19 slump with an RBI single.
Johan Oviedo placed on 15-day injured list due to a pitching elbow strain.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots begin their offseason workouts on April 20 with conditioning drills, meetings, and rehab work.
OTAs for the Patriots will take place on May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory minicamp is scheduled for June 15-17, concluding the Patriots' voluntary offseason program.
